items.headline: Nigerian airstrike kills over 100 civilians at market while targeting militants
items.body: A military strike aimed at jihadist rebels hit a village market in northeastern Yobe state. Amnesty International called for an independent investigation, noting over 500 civilians have died in similar misfires since 2017.

items.headline: Libya approves unified national budget for first time in 13 years
items.body: Rival legislative bodies endorsed a combined spending plan totaling roughly 30 billion dollars. The agreement comes as European demand for Libyan crude rises amid disruptions to Gulf oil routes.

items.headline: China Evergrande founder pleads guilty to fraud charges after two-day trial
items.body: Hui Ka Yan admitted to fundraising fraud, embezzlement, and bribery in a Shenzhen court. His company defaulted in 2021 on most of 300 billion dollars in liabilities, emblematic of China's property sector crisis.

items.headline: Maldives voters reject referendum to align presidential and parliamentary elections
items.body: The constitutional amendment lost with nearly 69 percent voting against shortening the current parliamentary term. Opposition parties swept all five mayoral seats, widely seen as a verdict on government performance.
